flashcards.front: What is the main function of the human eye?
flashcards.back: It enables us to see the world and its colours.

flashcards.front: What part of the eye acts like a camera's lens?
What is the light-sensitive screen at the back of the eye called?
What is the transparent bulge at the front of the eye?
What part of the eye controls the amount of light entering?
What structure controls the size of the pupil?
What type of image does the eye lens form on the retina?
What do the light-sensitive cells in the retina do?
flashcards.back: They generate electrical signals when illuminated.

flashcards.front: What is the ability of the eye lens to adjust its focal length called?
What happens to the eye lens when focusing on distant objects?
flashcards.back: The ciliary muscles relax, making the lens thinner and increasing its focal length.

flashcards.front: What happens to the eye lens when focusing on near objects?
flashcards.back: The ciliary muscles contract, making the lens thicker and decreasing its focal length.

flashcards.front: What is the minimum distance at which objects can be seen distinctly?
flashcards.back: The least distance of distinct vision, about 25 cm for a normal eye.

flashcards.front: What is the farthest point an eye can see clearly called?
flashcards.back: The far point of the eye, which is infinity for a normal eye.

flashcards.front: What is the condition where the eye lens becomes milky and cloudy?
What are the three common refractive defects of vision mentioned?
